question_text: A piece of wood has a density of 0.7 g/cm³. Will the wood float or sink in water? Explain your answer in terms of density.
correct_answer: The wood will float. Water has a density of 1 g/cm³, and since the wood's density (0.7 g/cm³) is less than water's density, it is less dense and will float on the surface.
explanation: Objects less dense than water (density < 1 g/cm³) will float, while objects denser than water (density > 1 g/cm³) will sink. The wood is less dense so its particles are more spread out, making it buoyant enough to stay on top of the water.
